reasoning: Casey Newton predicted this on Hard Fork, likely because Waymo is rapidly expanding in Los Angeles and becoming a prominent cultural touchstone that SNL writers (who spend time in NY/LA) are experiencing firsthand. Self-driving car antics provide rich comedic material. However, SNL produces only around 20 episodes per year, and securing an entire skit dedicated to (or prominently featuring) Waymo requires it to stay culturally relevant or have a specific viral moment during a week the show is in production. The base rate for a specific tech company getting a dedicated skit in a single calendar year is relatively low, making this plausible but more likely to not happen than to happen.
